books.title: Darby O’Gill and the Good People
books.author: Herminie Templeton Kavanagh
books.summary: When Tipperary man Darby O’Gill is imprisoned by the fairies of Sleive-na-mon in their home under the hollow mountain, he starts a lasting friendship with their King Brian Connors.

books.title: The Defiant Agents
books.author: Andre Norton
books.summary: Rival colonies are stranded on an alien world, their memories mixed with those of their ancestors and pitted against each other by the Western and Soviet forces who sent them.

books.title: Twenty Years at Hull House
books.author: Jane Addams
books.summary: Jane Addams’ autobiographical account of the founding and operation of Hull House, one of America’s most famous settlement homes.

books.title: Queen Victoria
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: A sardonic and generally unflattering biography of Victoria Alexandrina, Queen of England.

books.title: The Secret of the Old Clock
books.author: Carolyn Keene
books.summary: The sixteen-year-old daughter of a prominent attorney undertakes an investigation to search for a missing will.

books.title: Ukridge Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of stories featuring P. G. Wodehouse’s charismatic character Stanley Featherstonehaugh Ukridge.

books.title: Edward II
books.author: Christopher Marlowe
books.summary: Outraged by the favoritism shown by King Edward II to a close confidant, his court plots against him.

books.title: The Princess and the Goblin
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A princess and her young friend valiantly fend off a goblin attack with help from a ghostly relative.

books.title: Those Barren Leaves
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.summary: An aristocrat summons a group of “intelligentsia” to vacation in her Italian mansion.

books.title: The Vampire
books.author: John William Polidori
books.summary: A London gentleman goes on an ill-fated trip with a mysterious and sinister newcomer.

books.title: Twelve Years a Slave
books.author: Solomon Northup
books.summary: The account of a free black man kidnapped in 1841 and sold into slavery, from which it took twelve years to free himself.

books.title: Monsieur Lecoq
books.author: Émile Gaboriau
books.summary: Monsieur Lecoq’s first case in which he tries to determine the identity of a man in custody for three murders.

books.title: The Land of Little Rain
books.author: Mary Austin
books.summary: Short stories and essays detailing the landscape and inhabitants of the American Southwest.

books.title: The Kural
books.author: Thiruvalluvar
books.summary: An Indian weaver-philosopher expounds on the subjects of righteousness, wealth, and love.

books.title: The Rubáiyát of Omar Khayyám
books.author: Omar Khayyám
books.summary: Three volumes of lyrical quatrains about the joys of living, the meaning of existence, and what it means to lead a happy life.

books.title: Vile Bodies
books.author: Evelyn Waugh
books.summary: A young man in Roaring Twenties London endures ups and downs in varied situations and relationships.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A collection of short stories by H. G. Wells in a variety of genres ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Vikram and the Vampire
books.author: Richard F. Burton
books.summary: King Vikram becomes trapped in a cycle of stories as he tries to capture a cunning vampire.

books.title: Siddhartha
books.author: Hermann Hesse
books.summary: A man embarks on a spiritual journey through India.

books.title: Recollections of Full Years
books.author: Helen Herron Taft
books.summary: Helen Herron Taft, wife of William Howard Taft, the twenty-seventh President of the United States, recalls her life married to one of America’s most influential political figures.
